components.name: disodium hydrogen phosphate (Na2HPO4)
components.source: stock:M9 standard (Sambrook/CSH M9 salts, 5X = 64 g/L Na2HPO4·7H2O)
components.evidence: 'M9 minimal medium, which was prepared as previously described (Kotte et al, 2014)' (main_text); expanded to the standard M9-salts composition since neither the paper nor Kotte 2014 restate the phosphate concentrations.

components.name: monopotassium phosphate (KH2PO4)
components.source: stock:M9 standard (Sambrook/CSH M9 salts, 5X = 15 g/L KH2PO4)
components.evidence: 'M9 minimal medium, which was prepared as previously described (Kotte et al, 2014)' (main_text); standard M9-salts value.

components.name: sodium chloride (NaCl)
components.source: stock:M9 standard (Sambrook/CSH M9 salts, 5X = 2.5 g/L NaCl)
components.evidence: 'M9 minimal medium, which was prepared as previously described (Kotte et al, 2014)' (main_text); standard M9-salts value.

components.name: ammonium sulfate ((NH4)2SO4)
components.evidence: 'M9 medium with 1.5 g l-1 (NH4)2SO4, and 1 mg l-1 thiamin-HCl as the sole vitamin, was used.' (cited base-medium reference Kotte et al 2014). Replaces the NH4Cl of standard M9; NH4+ ~= 22.7 mM.

components.name: magnesium sulfate (MgSO4)
components.source: stock:M9 standard (Sambrook/CSH; 2 ml of 1 M MgSO4 per L)
components.evidence: 'M9 minimal medium, which was prepared as previously described (Kotte et al, 2014)' (main_text); standard M9 addition.

components.name: calcium chloride (CaCl2)
components.source: stock:M9 standard (Sambrook/CSH; 100 ul of 1 M CaCl2 per L)
components.evidence: 'M9 minimal medium, which was prepared as previously described (Kotte et al, 2014)' (main_text); standard M9 addition.

components.name: thiamine-HCl (vitamin B1)
components.evidence: 'M9 medium with 1.5 g l-1 (NH4)2SO4, and 1 mg l-1 thiamin-HCl as the sole vitamin, was used.' (cited base-medium reference).

components.name: fumarate (added as fumaric acid, pH-adjusted to 7 with NaOH -> sodium fumarate)
components.evidence: 'M9 medium was supplemented with a carbon source to a final concentration of 5 g l-1 glucose or 2 g l-1 fumarate, unless indicated otherwise. The carbon source stock solutions were made by dissolving the carbon source in demineralized water, adjusting the pH to 7 with NaOH or HCl'. conc_mM computed on a fumaric-acid basis (MW 116.07); ~12.3 mM if weighed as disodium fumarate.

media.base_medium: M9 minimal medium (standard Sambrook/CSH M9 salts) with (NH4)2SO4 as N source and thiamine-HCl as sole vitamin, per Kotte et al 2014

sources_chased.what: base recipe cited as 'prepared as previously described (Kotte et al, 2014)' = Mol Syst Biol 10:736, PMID 24987115, PMC4299493
standard M9-salts composition (phosphates, NaCl, MgSO4, CaCl2) since neither paper restates them
sources_chased.how: WebSearch/WebFetch canonical M9 (Sambrook / CSH Protocols M9 salts 5X)

missing_before: N source is (NH4)2SO4 (adds sulfate), not NH4Cl
media.notes: Aerobic shake-flask cultivation (50 ml in 500-ml flask, 37C, 300 rpm). The base M9 is standard Sambrook/CSH salts; the two documented modifications from Kotte 2014 are (NH4)2SO4 as N source and thiamine-HCl (1 mg/L) as the ONLY vitamin. IMPORTANT: standard M9 contains NO trace-element/micronutrient solution, so the Fe2+/Fe3+, Mn2+, Zn2+, Cu2+, Co2+, molybdate and Ni2+ exchanges in the current model list are genome-scale-model default open exchanges, NOT defined medium constituents; they are not supported by any cited recipe and should not be treated as a recipe trace mix.

components.name: disodium hydrogen phosphate (Na2HPO4)
components.evidence: 'cells switched to M9 medium without carbon source' (main_text); same M9-salts base 'prepared as previously described (Kotte et al, 2014)'.

components.name: monopotassium phosphate (KH2PO4)
components.evidence: Standard M9-salts value; base medium 'prepared as previously described (Kotte et al, 2014)'.

components.name: ammonium sulfate ((NH4)2SO4)
components.evidence: 'M9 medium with 1.5 g l-1 (NH4)2SO4 ... was used.' Carbon starvation only; nitrogen source retained.

components.name: thiamine-HCl (vitamin B1)
components.evidence: '... and 1 mg l-1 thiamin-HCl as the sole vitamin, was used.'

media.base_medium: M9 minimal medium (standard Sambrook/CSH M9 salts) with (NH4)2SO4 as N source and thiamine-HCl as sole vitamin, per Kotte et al 2014 — carbon source omitted (starvation)

sources_chased.what: base recipe cited as Kotte et al 2014 (PMC4299493)

missing_before: defined M9 salts (Na2HPO4, KH2PO4, NaCl, MgSO4, CaCl2)
media.notes: Same M9 base as the fumarate medium but WITHOUT any carbon source (glucose-to-none shift, starvation) — 'cells switched to M9 medium without carbon source'. Correctly carbon-free; N/P/salts and thiamine retained. As above, the Fe/Mn/Zn/Cu/Co/Mo/Ni exchanges are model defaults, not defined medium trace elements (standard M9 has no trace-element mix).
